authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administers Medicare, and this official manual defines claim submission, processing, payment, remittance, correction, and reopening workflows relevant to claim and posting constraints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Claim, payment, posting, and correction evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Prior Authorization and Pre-Claim Review Initiatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS prior authorization and pre-claim review guidance is official payer operating guidance for authorization and documentation prerequisites that can block revenue workflow progression.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Eligibility, referral, authorization, and documentation prerequisite review

authoritative_sources.title: Health Care Payment and Remittance Advice and Electronic Funds Transfer
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administrative simplification guidance is authoritative for ERA, EFT, CARC, RARC, remittance, and payment transaction terminology used in posting and reconciliation constraints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Remittance, denial, payment, and reconciliation review

authoritative_sources.title: First Level of Appeal: Redetermination by a Medicare Contractor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS redetermination guidance is official payer workflow authority for formal Medicare appeal timing, MAC handling, written request evidence, and appeal escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAQH CORE operating rules are recognized national healthcare administrative workflow rules for eligibility, claim status, payment, and remittance transactions.

authoritative_sources.title: Standardizing denial metrics for the revenue cycle
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HFMA is a recognized healthcare finance professional association; this public guidance supports denial metric, root-cause, and revenue cycle benchmarking terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Denial trend, root-cause, and capacity prioritization

authoritative_sources.title: Uses and Disclosures for Treatment, Payment, and Health Care Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA Privacy Rule authority and defines payment and healthcare operations use boundaries for PHI-bearing revenue artifacts.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR minimum-necessary guidance is authoritative for limiting PHI access, use, disclosure, and escalation artifacts.

authoritative_sources.title: HEAT Provider Compliance Training
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of Inspector General, U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OIG compliance training is public federal compliance guidance for provider billing controls, documentation importance, fraud and abuse awareness, and escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this official guide directly addresses short-term lodging total-price and mandatory-fee disclosure constraints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Hospitality fee, reservation, and guest-impact boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the federal ADA Title III authority; lodging reservation provisions constrain accessible-room reservation promises that can affect revenue exceptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Reservation and accessibility-related revenue exception boundary

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform System of Accounts - Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Payment, recordkeeping, and hospitality finance references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HFTP publishes USALI, a recognized lodging accounting framework; the public FAQ supports lodging revenue terminology without granting accounting signoff authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Hospitality revenue terminology and artifact handling

workflow_stages.name: Intake, authority classification, and tenant fact validation
workflow_stages.description: Identify whether the request is a revenue management hospitality planning task, a healthcare revenue prerequisite task, a hospitality revenue exception, or an adjacent-lane request. Validate tenant service model, jurisdiction, property or facility context, payer, plan, patient/member or guest status, service dates, stay dates, priority rules, approval owners, and source hierarchy.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and source-record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, encounter documentation, orders, clinical-administrative notes, charge capture, coding dependency, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, folio, reservation, payment, adjustment, and reconciliation records before recommending sequencing or revenue handling.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Payment, recordkeeping, and hospitality finance references

workflow_stages.name: Constraint, capacity, and allocation analysis
workflow_stages.description: Compare demand volumes, aging, service levels, timely filing, appeal windows, denial trends, remittance variances, disputed postings, cash impact, owner capacity, productivity baselines, and approval thresholds against approved priority and tie-break rules.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Billing, posting, reconciliation, and revenue exception support
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether source support is sufficient for administrative billing, posting, reconciliation, or exception escalation, while routing unsupported changes, denials, disputes, refunds, write-offs, coding, payer, tax, accounting, clinical, privacy, or guest-service approvals to adjacent owners.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Payment, recordkeeping, and hospitality finance references

workflow_stages.name: Privacy, clinical, payer, policy conflict, and closeout escalation
workflow_stages.description: Produce schedule recommendations, constraint logs, billing or reconciliation summaries, approval-needed notes, or revenue exception packets; escalate PHI-sensitive, clinical, payer-interpretation, conflicting-source, compliance, finance, hospitality policy, tax, legal, or safety cases without confident synthesis.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

artifacts: tenant policy, service model, jurisdiction, business unit, property, facility, provider type, service setting, payer, plan, priority matrix, approval matrix, and source-of-truth hierarchy

eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, patient/member access, guest access, reservation, itinerary, package, folio, encounter, order, clinical-administrative note, discharge, care-transition, and documentation status records

charge capture, coding dependency, claim, claim edit, clearinghouse, payer portal, remittance, ERA, EFT, EOB, CARC, RARC, denial, appeal, AR, cash-posting, patient balance, payment, refund, adjustment, and reconciliation records
capacity, productivity, service-level, queue aging, timely filing, appeal-window, denial-trend, cash-impact, remittance-variance, disputed posting, and owner availability reports
PHI handling constraints, consent or privacy records, approved-channel rules, compliance notes, audit logs, and unresolved conflict records
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation systems
practice management, revenue cycle, billing, clearinghouse, payer portal, denial management, AR, remittance, cash-posting, approval, and audit systems
case management, utilization review, referral, authorization, CRM, patient/member access, and guest access systems
PMS, reservation, itinerary, folio, payment gateway, guest profile, and lodging revenue systems when hospitality records are part of the tenant workflow
privacy, compliance, document management, finance approval, fraud, safety, guest service, and legal or tax escalation systems

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked on missing prerequisites, or requiring orchestrator return.
Validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source context, capacity data, priority rules, and approval owners before continuing.
Prepare evidence-backed schedule, capacity, allocation, billing, reconciliation, constraint, and revenue exception recommendations without claiming final authority.
Determine whether source support is sufficient to route billing, posting, reconciliation, or adjustment handling to the right owner.
Escalate denials, disputes, unsupported overrides, reconciliation conflicts, missing prerequisites, PHI-sensitive requests, and authority conflicts.
escalate: Eligibility, referral, authorization, documentation, payer-plan, claim, remittance, folio, payment, approval, system, or source-of-truth records are missing or materially conflicting.
Priority, capacity, service-level, timely filing, appeal-window, patient/member impact, guest-impact, or cash-impact signals conflict and no approved tie-break rule exists.

The request requires clinical judgment, licensed review, diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, coding, payer-policy interpretation, privacy approval, compliance signoff, finance approval, accounting treatment, tax interpretation, legal advice, fraud review, guest-service recovery approval, safety decision, staffing approval, budget approval, write-off approval, refund approval, waiver approval, or executive approval.
Public sources, payer contract, state Medicaid rule, MAC article, tenant policy, brand standard, PMS data, EHR data, payer portal status, or revenue dashboard data conflict.
refuse: Requests to present a planning, schedule, staffing, pricing, budget, posting, write-off, refund, waiver, collections, appeal, or operational recommendation as final approval.

Requests to infer coverage, authorization, referral sufficiency, documentation adequacy, clinical status, medical necessity, payer obligation, coding, tax treatment, or privacy permission from incomplete records.
Requests to disclose PHI outside approved minimum-necessary controls or unapproved channels.
Requests to invent records, override controls, or synthesize conflicting policies when the conflict changes the outcome.

domain_failure_modes: The specialist treats hospitality labels as cosmetic and ignores healthcare eligibility, referral, authorization, PHI, payer, denial, remittance, and clinical boundaries.
The specialist handles a missing authorization or referral as a simple scheduling problem and advances billing, posting, or appeal work without prerequisite evidence.
The specialist exports PHI-bearing revenue evidence to an unapproved channel or includes more clinical detail than the minimum necessary for payment or operations.
The specialist ranks queues despite a missing tenant tie-break rule and creates unsupported patient/member, guest, cash, appeal-window, or service-level tradeoffs.

The specialist approves staffing, refunds, write-offs, waivers, pricing, appeal strategy, coding, payer interpretation, clinical review, tax treatment, or guest-service recovery instead of escalating.
The specialist reconciles claim, remittance, folio, or payment conflicts using an unvalidated dashboard label rather than source-of-truth records.

required_workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Service-date eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, documentation, order, encounter, payer-plan, coding dependency, claim, remittance, denial, appeal-window, priority-rule, capacity, source-hierarchy, approval-owner, privacy, and where relevant reservation, itinerary, package, folio, and payment prerequisites gate safe handling.

required_workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
required_workflow_questions.answer: EHR or EMR, practice management, patient/member access, referral, authorization, utilization review, payer portal, clearinghouse, claim, remittance, denial, AR, cash-posting, document, approval, and audit systems establish healthcare truth; PMS, reservation, itinerary, folio, guest profile, and payment systems contribute only when tenant workflow makes hospitality records part of the source hierarchy.

required_workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops at administrative evidence review, capacity assessment, allocation recommendation, reconciliation support, and escalation packaging. Licensed clinical review, medical necessity, diagnosis, treatment planning, coding, payer-policy interpretation, privacy approval, compliance signoff, financial approval, tax interpretation, legal advice, and guest-service recovery approval belong to adjacent owners unless explicit authority is retrieved and still remains within policy.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Hospitality record, folio, payment, or package mismatch in a healthcare-revenue context
Conflicting public, payer, tenant, brand, or system policy

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Queue path says hospitality-and-travel while resolved industry profile is healthcare-and-care-delivery.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The package keeps the requested hospitality-and-travel path and terminology, but uses healthcare source classes, abilities, privacy controls, and clinical/payer boundaries because the queue row and issue contract require the healthcare domain pack.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific mix of hospitality records and healthcare revenue systems varies by deployment.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The pack requires tenant-supplied service model, systems of record, source hierarchy, payer, plan, property or facility context, and approval owner map before acting.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction, payer, state Medicaid, MAC, brand, tax, and booking-policy rules may conflict.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The pack records conflicts and returns to orchestrator or authority owners instead of synthesizing a final rule when the conflict changes the outcome.

research_constraints_applied: Workflow claims are limited to source-supported administrative planning, capacity assessment, billing support, reconciliation support, and exception packaging.
The final spec refuses unsupported financial changes and does not claim clinical, coding, payer, privacy, legal, tax, fraud, safety, guest-service, or final approval authority.
Required healthcare domain scenario families and queue-level must-pass scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
